checks.finding: REQ-006 through REQ-017 define observable artifacts, preserved behavior, command interfaces, validation failures, review bindings, and approval states; AC-001 through AC-016 and the behavioral contracts provide corresponding measurable outcomes.
Every goal REQ-006 through REQ-017 is mapped to at least one acceptance criterion in the acceptance-test matrix, including review-boundary and approval-governance goals through AC-015 and AC-016.

AC-001 through AC-016 specify inspectable document content, stable diagnostics, exit behavior, generated or preserved artifacts, manifest hashes, version consistency, and task-state transitions; the behavioral contracts further identify concrete inputs and expected results.

The Non-goals explicitly exclude direct Figma integration, automated visualization invocation and rendering, a new review stage, quality-gate or sdd-ship changes, LITE model changes, and a dedicated backend layer file; REQ-013 and the workflows also bound LITE behavior.

REQ-012 defines exact POSIX and PowerShell interfaces, slug validation, failure text, exit status, and traversal containment; REQ-016 defines canonical hash-bound review inputs; REQ-017 preserves the human or signed-sdd-sudo approval boundary; compatibility, versioning, and overwrite constraints are also explicit.

The identified risks have planned validation surfaces: AC-015 covers canonical-path and tamper rejection, AC-011 and TEST-019 cover legacy preflight compatibility, AC-009 and TEST-017 cover N/A layer handling, and AC-014 covers metadata drift.
